scenes.title: Messy Loan File Intake
scenes.editIntent: Open with the pain state — a real loan file folder with 12 documents, no version control, and zero separation. Make the auditor questions feel visceral and familiar to anyone who has worked in mortgage compliance.

blocks.text: This is what a loan folder actually looks like when it arrives.
Twelve documents. No clear separation. No version control.

scenes.title: Document Identity and Classification
scenes.editIntent: Show order emerging from chaos. The system automatically classifies every document, assigns a traceable ID, and groups version conflicts — no human instruction needed. Focus on the transformation from 12 unordered files to 8 tagged, categorized records.

blocks.text: The system does not need to be told what to look for.
It identifies each document, assigns a traceable ID, and surfaces every version conflict automatically.

scenes.title: Used in Closing?
scenes.editIntent: Show the human review step where a reviewer confirms which version of each conflicting document was actually used at closing. The interaction should feel lightweight and decisive — not a heavy form, but a fast confirmation layer on top of machine-surfaced conflicts.

blocks.text: The system found the version conflicts.
Now the reviewer confirms what actually mattered.

scenes.title: Separation of Record
scenes.editIntent: The aha moment — every document has a permanent, governed home. Three clean buckets: closing package, inactive archive, and exception queue. Nothing is mixed together. This is what a governed process looks like compared to a flat folder.

blocks.text: Every document now has a home.

scenes.title: Audit-Ready Proof
scenes.editIntent: Close with calm confidence. The file can explain itself — a complete chain of custody, all decisions logged, all counts accurate, and a one-click audit report. This scene should feel like the opposite of the opening chaos scene.

blocks.text: At any moment, the file can explain itself.
What was used. What was excluded. Who decided. Why.
